title: Alertmanager routing model
description: Alertmanager groups, routes, inhibits, and silences alerts via a routing tree keyed on labels.
body: Use when reasoning about how alerts become notifications. Alertmanager takes alerts from Prometheus and applies a routing tree: it groups by labels, routes to receivers by matchers, inhibits alerts when a higher-priority one is active, and honors silences. Do not assume one alert equals one page — grouping and inhibition shape the output. Actions: design the routing tree and inhibition rules deliberately. Validation signals include grouped notifications per incident. Risk boundary: a broad inhibition or silence can suppress an unrelated real alert, so scope matchers tightly.
